item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ONE YEAR LATER, CACK!

Aspartame is a common ingredient in diet soda, and is hated by the regular sodas because it is a sugar substitute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one has me kind of confused- the sweet stuff if alcohol, which isn't usually very sweet, but aspartame, the sciency name for sugar is considered a challenge. Can I get some explaineratings, cack?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great SCP, quite unpredictable in every single aspect. Very straightforward and did not take too long to read through specific parts.
Imagining the soda cans bullying smaller brands of cans, with other soda cans having a drinking contest in the background, all while Party Rock Anthem by LMFAO is playing was funny to imagine in my head. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, too, despise the taste of sugar-free soda. That dry bitter aftertaste, that over-the-top sweetness…

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually had an idea for a tale surrounding this. The idea is that SCP-3905 is one of the least classified/well-known SCPs in the Foundation, because it's apparently harmless and serves as a good introduction to the weird world of the supernatural for new researchers. I was going to write it from the perspective of the researchers on the ship, with the general tone being "I can't believe I went to college for over a decade to spend my days watching fucking soda cans dance". Would also include more information about the soda cans and their social structure.

Unfortunately, I'm not too good at writing prose, as evidenced by the fact that my highest-rated tale is +16 and is written with emails.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those articles that is silly, but skirts enough into flat out weird to keep the SCP vibe. I especially think the treatment of Diet Soda Cans was an interesting touch, and I would very much be down to check out a tale or two to explore more of this article and the cans there in.

+1. I dig it.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Aye. This was pretty quirky without overstaying its welcome, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suggestion implemented, hopefully it's allowed as a minor edit under contest rules

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't convinced at first, but the whole social hierarchy won me over.

That said, I would really suggest changing "right" in the image caption to "inset". Looking at the image before reading the article, it appeared you had very poorly photoshopped some kind of wall over the water. I had to open the image and really squint to figure it out with the old man eyes I now apparently possess. D:

item.title: Re: Jam Con #2
item.content:encoded: Cool. I fixed up the image code and got the image down to 351 kb. The larger version is still available in Files.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the phrasing/grammar corrections; will get them in after the contest.

I think thedeadlymoose got it right with "A mainlist SCP can be funny, it just can't be a joke." This is stupid, but could definitely exist as an anomaly in universe and doesn't sacrifice any containment conventions for humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a lot of fun — it skirts really close to -J material, but a few lines in it were too great for me not to ultimately decide to upvote.

FEEDBACK
– hide block

In all other instances where trash is deposited, SCP-3905 behaves identically to a non-anomalous trash can.

This is a pretty great line (because it's both hilarious and something I can legitiately imagine the Foundation sincerely writing).

SCP-3905-B instances display signs of sapience

…you actually used the right term, too (as opposed to 'sentience'). O_O

There's a few spots here I think you can tighten up (probably after the contest) that I wanted to mention; feel free to take any of it or leave any of it — I think this skip is pretty great as is.

SCP-3905-B instances anomalously possess the physical dexterity to can perform many actions that would normally require arms, legs, or a greater height, such as limboing.

Given that this is a list of anomalous properties exhibited by SCP-3905-B instances, I think you can just cut all this out and say this is what they can (HA!) do.

(e.g. “Feeling Hot Hot Hot” by The Merrymen, “Let’s Get It Started” by the Black Eyed Peas, etc)

That 'etc' feels a little unclinical to me; I think the following would be just slightly stronger:

(Examples include "Feeling Hot Hot Hot" by The Merrymen, and "Let's Get It Started" by the Black Eyed Peas).

On the western end of SCP-3905-A, a plastic tube protruding from the ground and labeled “THE SWEET STUFF” continuously produces a substance believed by SCP-3905-A researchers to be alcohol.

I think you can take out the crossed out portion, because really — who other than the researchers would be stating this belief?

SCP-3905-B instances apparently violate the laws of space-time while consuming the alcohol, as they have been observed to repeatedly consume far more than would be able to fit into a 355ml can without spilling any contents out.

I feel like this can be made much more simple and clinical (also, you started the previous sentence with SCP-3905-B, so I'd avoid it in the next sentence):

These instances have been observed consuming volumes of liquid far greater than what a non-anomalous 355ml aluminum can would allow.

This area is frequented less often than the western one, and SCP-3905-B instances who consume the contents of the tube almost immediately expel it from themselves while showing apparent signs of expressing displeasure.

SCP-3905-B instances has have been observed to have a rough social structure abide by a social hierarchy based on the soda brands represented by SCP-3905-B their brands. Well-known non-diet sodas (Pepsi, Coke, RC Cola) apparently issue orders and occasionally physically harm assault less popular or and generic brands such as refreshe,. Examples of this behavior include usually by breaking off their soda tabs or and involuntarily pouring forcing the contents of the eastern plastic tube into them.

Okay, that's a lot of edits there I know. Not all of that is super important, but 'involuntarily' seems like the wrong word, and there definitely needs to be an 'on' in front of 'based'.

These soda cans are inert and do not display the same anomalous properties that SCP-3905-B instances do. To date, no diet or sugar-free cans have become instances of SCP-3905-B.

I feel like you can get this across much more simply:

These aluminum cans appear to be non-anomalous.

Like, you don't need that last line — it's pretty strongly implied, and I feel like 'non-anomalous' is a good word to end on.

I really liked this — I know I threw a lot of things in there I think you should change, but keep in mind, I don't think any of those changes are necessary — they're just things that I think would significantly tighten it.

Good luck with the contest!

item.title: Re: Jam Con #2
item.content:encoded: Yeah, sure, if you want. :P This is the original image.

item.title: Re: Jam Con #2
item.content:encoded: …still pretty big though, for something not much bigger than 300px in width. Want me to shrink it more?

item.title: Re: Jam Con #2
item.content:encoded:
Cool!

I'll read this in just a bit, but first things first: Would you mind shrinking that image down? It's pretty huge (4+ megs!), and devours bandwidth.

item.title: Jam Con #2
item.content:encoded:
So, I had some difficulty writing an SCP for the second prompt, and ended up playing free association. This contest really is "squeezing the inspiration" out of me XD

Images are both from Pixabay and have been released under CC0 (public domain): 1, 2
